sections.content:
The Coordinate Plane

A coordinate plane (Cartesian plane) has two axes: the horizontal x-axis and the vertical y-axis, crossing at the origin (0, 0). Every point is described by an ordered pair (x, y). Moving right increases x; moving up increases y; left and down give negative values.

Plotting points precisely and reading coordinates from a graph are fundamental skills — all of functions, statistics, and calculus are communicated through graphs.
🔑 Straight-line equation: y = mx + c, where m is the gradient (slope) and c is the y-intercept (where the line crosses the y-axis). Gradient = rise ÷ run = (y₂ − y₁)/(x₂ − x₁).

Linear Graphs

Any equation of the form y = mx + c produces a straight line. To sketch it: (1) plot the y-intercept (0, c); (2) use the gradient to find a second point (go 1 right, m up); (3) draw through both points.

Two lines are parallel if they have the same gradient. Two lines are perpendicular if their gradients multiply to −1 (e.g. gradient 2 is perpendicular to gradient −½).

To find the equation of a line through two points, first calculate m, then substitute one point into y = mx + c to find c.

Quadratic Graphs

The graph of y = ax² + bx + c is a parabola — a smooth U-shape (or ∩ if a < 0). Key features:

  • Vertex — the minimum (or maximum) point, at x = −b/(2a)
  • Axis of symmetry — vertical line x = −b/(2a)
  • y-intercept — always at (0, c)
  • x-intercepts (roots) — where the parabola crosses the x-axis; found by solving ax² + bx + c = 0

Functions

A function is a rule that turns every input into exactly one output. We write f(x) to mean 'the function f, applied to x'. For example, f(x) = 2x + 1 means: double the input and add 1. So f(3) = 7.

The set of valid inputs is the domain; the set of possible outputs is the range. You can't divide by zero, and you can't take the square root of a negative (in real numbers) — these restrictions define the domain.

questions.question: What is the gradient of the line y = −3x + 7?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, m is the gradient. Here m = −3. The 7 is the y-intercept, not the gradient.

questions.question: For the parabola y = x² − 4x + 3, what is the axis of symmetry?
If f(x) = x² + 2x, what is f(−3)?
Two lines have gradients 4 and k. They are perpendicular. What is k?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular gradients multiply to −1: 4 × k = −1, so k = −¼.
